mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above a 0.5 percentage point threshold signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, which compounds into sustained equity appreciation over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns specifically during recovery phases, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds a 3.0% annualized threshold, anticipated central bank tightening compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL crosses the 3.0% threshold, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals manufacturing contraction and impending earnings downgrades, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal generates positive returns in more than 60% of observed instances.
